title: Running 128 Coding Agents at Once
summary: Cursor's Sam Whitmore discusses scaling and orchestrating coding agents with Baseten's Charlie O'Neill and Harry Partridge. The conversation covers running agents at scale (by the hundreds), enabling inter-agent communication and review workflows, and building coordinated multi-agent systems rather than simple parallel task management.
writeup: Cursor's Sam Whitmore joins Baseten's Charlie O'Neill and Harry Partridge to discuss scaling and orchestrating coding agents, including running them by the hundreds. The conversation distinguishes coordinated multi-agent systems from simple parallel task management, covering inter-agent communication and review workflows. The takeaway is that orchestration at this scale depends on agents reviewing and communicating with one another rather than executing tasks in isolation.
